meta.name: ASRS Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale
meta.fullName: Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale v1.1 Screener
meta.description: The ASRS v1.1 Screener is the Part A of the Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale developed by the World Health Organization (WHO), containing 6 of the most predictive items. Based on DSM-IV-TR criteria, it is used for rapid identification of adults who may have ADHD.
meta.author: Kessler et al. (2005)
meta.reference: Kessler RC, Adler L, Ames M, et al. The World Health Organization Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale (ASRS): a short screening scale for use in the general population. Psychol Med. 2005;35(2):245-256.
meta.instruction: Please answer the questions below, rating yourself on each of the criteria shown using the scale. As you answer each question, select the option that best describes how you have felt and conducted yourself over the past 6 months.

questions.text: How often do you have trouble wrapping up the final details of a project, once the challenging parts have been done?
How often do you have difficulty getting things in order when you have to do a task that requires organization?
How often do you have problems remembering appointments or obligations?
When you have a task that requires a lot of thought, how often do you avoid or delay getting started?
How often do you fidget or squirm with your hands or feet when you have to sit down for a long time?
How often do you feel overly active and compelled to do things, like you were driven by a motor?

ranges.label: Normal Range
ranges.description: Your ADHD screening score is within the normal range. Adult ADHD is unlikely.
suggestions: No further ADHD assessment is currently needed
If you still have attention or activity concerns, try time management techniques
Maintaining a healthy lifestyle helps with focus and concentration

ranges.label: Possible ADHD
ranges.description: Your score suggests possible ADHD symptoms. Further evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Consider consulting a professional for a comprehensive ADHD evaluation
Learn about ADHD to improve self-understanding
Try using planners and reminder tools for daily organization
Regular exercise can help improve attention and hyperactivity symptoms

ranges.label: Likely ADHD
ranges.description: Your score is elevated and ADHD symptoms are likely present. Professional evaluation is strongly recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek evaluation from a psychiatrist or psychologist
ADHD is a treatable condition — both medication and behavioral interventions can help
While awaiting evaluation, learn ADHD coping strategies
Build external reminder and organizational systems for daily life
Ensure adequate sleep and regular exercise
